zh.lifeSummary: 當Robert Bertram Baron of Bothal於大約1160年出生在英國英格蘭諾圖波藍德波塔爾時，他的父親Baron Richard de Bertram26歲，母親Baroness Menebell Gisulf23歲。他於大約1192年與Mabel de Clare結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於大約1203年享年45歲。

nameMeanings.name: Bertram
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German, English (mainly in southeastern Scotland, of Norman origin), French, and Danish: from the ancient Germanic personal name Bertram, composed of the elements berht ‘bright, famous’ + hramn ‘raven’. The raven was the bird of Odin, king of the gods, in ancient Germanic mythology. The personal name was common in France throughout the Middle Ages (especially in the form Bertrand ), where its popularity was increased by the fame of the troubadour Bertrand de Born (c. 1140–1214). The name was taken to England by the Normans in the forms Bertran(d), Bertram, and Bartram.

variants.name: Beltrami
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Dante, Dino, Marco, Pio, Sal, Tullio.
Italian: patronymic or plural form of the personal name Beltrame, from an ancient Germanic name composed of the elements berht ‘bright, famous’ + hramn ‘raven’. See also Bertram .

variants.name: Bartram
meanings: English (of Norman origin) and North German: variant of   Bertram  .

History: William Bartram, a Quaker, had a large farm near Darby, PA, when his eldest son, John, the first American botanist, was born in 1699. John conducted botanical experiments at his own farm in Kingsessing, PA, near Philadelphia.

variants.name: Bertman
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Frieda, Wilhelm.
Americanized form of German Bertmann: altered form (by folk etymology) of Bertram .

variants.name: Bethke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Eldor, Ewald, Otto, Rudi, Wilhelmina.
North German: from a pet form of the ancient Germanic personal names   Bertram   or   Berthold  . See also   Beth  .

variants.name: Bertrand
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Marcel, Andre, Armand, Monique, Francois, Gilles, Raoul, Emile, Herve, Jacques, Jean-Marie, Odette.

French, Walloon, and West Indian (mainly Haiti): from the French personal name Bertrand, a variant of Bertram , coined by folk etymology under the influence of the present participle endings -and, -ant.
History: This surname is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America.

variants.name: Beltran
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Manuel, Carlos, Jesus, Luis, Pedro, Jorge, Mario, Ricardo, Francisco, Rafael.
Spanish (Beltrán), Catalan, and southern French: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements berht ‘bright, famous’ + hramn ‘raven’. See also Bertram .

variants.name: Beltramo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo.
Italian: from the medieval personal name Beltramo, of ancient Germanic origin (see Bertram ).

variants.name: Bert
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Philibert, Pierre.
German, French, and Polish: from a short form of various compound personal names formed with ancient Germanic berht ‘bright, famous’ (see for example Bertram ).
English: from the Middle English female personal name Berte (Old French Berte, from ancient Germanic Berta ).

variants.name: Battermann
meanings: German:
possibly an occupational name for a merchant or peddler, from Bartermann.
alternatively, perhaps from a short form of the personal name Bertram + Middle High German man ‘man’.

variants.name: Bertran
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Jorge, Eduardo, Enrique, Joaquin, Ricardo, Aleida, Ana, Ana Maria, Elena, Felipe, Jeronimo.  French Andre.
French, Occitan, and Catalan: variant of   Bertram   or   Bertrand  .

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

en.lifeSummary: When Robert Bertram Baron of Bothal was born about 1160, in Bothal, Northumberland, England, United Kingdom, his father, Baron Richard de Bertram, was 26 and his mother, Baroness Menebell Gisulf, was 23. He married Mabel de Clare about 1192. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died about 1203, at the age of 45.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Robert Bertram Baron of Bothal est né environ 1160, à Bothal, Northumberland, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Baron Richard de Bertram, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Baroness Menebell Gisulf, avait 23 ans. Il a épousé Mabel de Clare environ 1192. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé environ 1203, à l’âge de 45 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Robert Bertram Baron of Bothal um 1160 in Bothal, Northumberland, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Baron Richard de Bertram 26 und seine Mutter Baroness Menebell Gisulf 23. Er hat um 1192 Mabel de Clare geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb um 1203 im Alter von 45 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Robert Bertram Baron of Bothal è nato il circa 1160 nel Bothal, Northumberland, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Baron Richard de Bertram, aveva 26 anni e il suo madre, Baroness Menebell Gisulf, aveva 23 anni. Lui sposato Mabel de Clare circa 1192. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì circa 1203, all'età di 45 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Robert Bertram Baron of Bothal(이)가 대략 1160년 영국 잉글랜드 노르스벌랜드 벗할에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Baron Richard de Bertram(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Baroness Menebell Gisulf(은)는 23세였다. 그는 대략 1192년 Mabel de Clare(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 대략 1203년 45세의 나이로 사망했다.
